LinkedIn Homepage FAQ | LinkedIn Help
LinkedIn Homepage FAQ
Last updated: 3 years ago
The LinkedIn experience was designed so you can easily surface ideas, drive conversations, and discover topics you care about. When you sign into LinkedIn, you land on your homepage, which gives you access to your feed, connections, profile, messages, and notifications. Your LinkedIn Feed contains posts from your network, companies you follow, and more.

Check out some of these frequently asked questions about the LinkedIn Homepage.
What features are within the top navigation bar and how do I access features I don't see in the navigation?
The LinkedIn.com navigation bar is a simple menu of tabs located at the top of the LinkedIn homepage that makes it easy to access various features quickly. The menu includes My Network, Jobs, Messaging, Notifications, Me, and Work.
My Network allows you to manage your connections, invitations, and groups,

Jobs enable you to search and post job listings.
Messaging is where you can access your messages
Notifications let you view updates about your connections.
Me is where you can view and edit your profile, manage account settings, and access the Help Center.
To access additional features and products:
Click the
Work
icon at the upper-right corner of your LinkedIn homepage to launch other products that matter to you, like LinkedIn Learning or Insights.
Learn more
LinkedIn.com navigation bar
.

Learn more
LinkedIn.com navigation bar
.
How do I search on LinkedIn?
The LinkedIn
Search
bar at the top of every page allows you to search for people, jobs, posts, companies, groups, and schools. You can click any of the search suggestions that appear in the dropdown list as you type or generate your search to see the full results. To search for jobs, you can click the
Jobs
icon in the homepage and search for jobs in the Jobs homepage.
Learn more
Search on LinkedIn
Premium search filters

Search on LinkedIn
Premium search filters
- Available in LinkedIn Recruiter and Sales Navigator
Can I sort updates in my feed?
You can sort top and recent updates in your feed.
Learn more
Sort feed by Most Relevant and Most Recent posts
Best practices to customize what you see on your LinkedIn Feed
How do I customize my feed?

How do I customize my feed?
Your LinkedIn Feed can be customized by following or unfollowing people and companies, by hiding content in your feed that you don't want to see, and by reporting inappropriate content.
Learn more
Best practices to customize what you see on your LinkedIn Feed
How can I access my Settings & Privacy page?
To access your
Settings & Privacy
page, click the
Me
icon at the top of your LinkedIn homepage, then click
Settings & Privacy

Settings & Privacy
. This will take you to a page where you can customize your LinkedIn experience and adjust your privacy settings.
Learn more
Manage your account and privacy settings
.
How can I adjust the notifications I receive?
You can receive notifications about yourself, your activity, and your connections. These include alerts for your posts or shares, career opportunities, and updates about people in your network.
Learn more
Manage your LinkedIn notification updates

Manage your LinkedIn notification updates
How do I share an update or post?
You can use the share box at the top of your LinkedIn homepage to share your thoughts, articles or other content directly from your LinkedIn homepage.
Learn more
Post and share content on LinkedIn
How do I access and edit my LinkedIn Page?
LinkedIn Page admins can access the Pages they manage through an admin view that is respective to their assigned admin role. Admins don’t need a separate LinkedIn account login to access a Page.

Learn more
Access LinkedIn Pages admin view
Edit your LinkedIn Page
What is the difference between editing my profile and my public profile?
Your public profile appears when people search for you using a public search engine like Google, Yahoo!, Bing, etc. as well as on other third-party applications and platforms, subject to your privacy settings. You can change your public profile to turn on or off the sections you want visible on public search engines.

When you edit your profile, not all sections are visible automatically on your public profile. To customize which sections are visible, you must first go to your Public Profile page.
Learn more
LinkedIn public profile visibility
